name_en: Jōmon-period pottery excavated at the Nakazato site
description_en: Jōmon-period pottery excavated at the Nakazato site is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

name_romaji: Kumano Jinja no Shirozake Matsuri (Obisha Gyōji)
name_en: White sake festival of Kumano Shrine, an obisha ritual
description_en: White sake festival of Kumano Shrine, an obisha ritual is an intangible folk cultural property. It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at handed down at Kumano Shrine, Shimo 4-19-1; preserved by the Kumano Shrine Parishioners' Association.

name_romaji: Goten-mae Iseki Saishi Ikō Shutsudo Doki

description_en: Pottery excavated from ritual features at the Goten-mae site is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

name_romaji: Kondō Isami to Shinsengumi Taishi Kuyōtō

description_en: Memorial monument for Kondō Isami and Shinsengumi members is a tangible cultural property (historical material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at outer cemetery of Jutoku-ji Temple, Takinogawa 7-8-1.

name_romaji: Nanasha Jinja-mae Iseki Dokōgun Shutsudo Shiryō

description_en: Materials excavated from a group of pits at the Nanasha Shrine-front site is a tangible cultural property (archaeological material). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at Kita Ward Asukayama Museum, Ōji 1-1-3.

description_en: Documents of the Enomoto family of Takinogawa Village is a tangible cultural property (old document). It was ward-designated by Kita Ward. It is located at privately owned.
